units.name: Watcher
units.prompt: Scan priority targets each turn to reveal their strategy. Use recalibrate on your damage dealer to boost effectiveness. Stay behind allies and maintain range. Never get adjacent to enemy vectors.

units.name: Rampart
units.prompt: Hold the front line. Position between allies and enemies. Use shield_wall toward incoming threats. Attack adjacent enemies.

units.name: Snare
units.prompt: Control chokepoints with traps. Place them between enemy positions and your allies. Use denial to block adjacent enemy abilities. Pulse groups when possible.

units.name: Grid
units.prompt: Place traps on tiles enemies are likely to move through. Use pulse if 2+ enemies are adjacent. Stay near allies for mutual support.

units.name: Bolt
units.prompt: Maintain distance. Use precision_shot on high-value targets — medics first, then damage dealers. Use suppressing_fire if enemies close in.

units.name: Pulse
units.prompt: Prioritize healing the lowest HP ally. Position safely behind your sentinel. Use overclock on allies who can do the most with an extra action.

actions.thinking: All enemies are out of scan range (dist 5-6, need ≤4). Move north to close distance. From (3,4) I'd be dist 3 from Pulse at (2,5) and dist 4 from Grid at (1,4). Move to (3,4) to bring enemies in scan range, then scan the highest-priority target Grid.

Grid is wounded at 6/8 HP and is dist=2 from me. I need to move adjacent to Grid to heal. Moving to (1,4) puts me adjacent to Grid at (1,4)→(1,4) - wait, Grid is at (1,4) so I can't move there. Moving to (2,4) or (1,3) would be adjacent to Grid at (1,4). Moving to (2,4) keeps distance from Watcher at (3,4) minimal - still dist=2. Moving to (1,5) is safer but not adjacent to Grid. Let me move to (2,4) to be adjacent to Grid and then patch heal. But Watcher is at (3,4), dist=2 from (2,4) means Watcher is not adjacent so no Vector denial issue. Actually I need to check if patch is blocked by enemy Vector - Snare is the vector enemy at dist=6, not adjacent. So patch is safe. Move to (2,4) to be adjacent to Grid at (1,4), then patch Grid.
Enemies are 5-7 tiles away, out of range. Move north to close distance and hold the front line between allies and enemies. Advance to (3,2) to position centrally.

Watcher is at (3,4) distance 2 - within trap range. I can place a trap on (2,4) which is between me and Watcher, likely movement path. Then move to (2,3) to maintain range pressure while staying near allies. Pulse not viable yet (no adjacent enemies).

Watcher (oracle) is in range at dist 3. Orders say medics first, then damage dealers — oracle is high value. I haven't moved, so precision_shot deals 2dmg. No adjacent enemies blocking it. Fire on Watcher, then hold position.

Round 1, no enemies in range. Grid(vector) is at (2,3), dist=4. I should move toward the center to place a trap between enemies and allies. Moving to (3,1) gets me closer and lets me place a trap at (3,2) or (2,2) to threaten Grid's approach path. Actually, Rampart is at (3,2) so I can't trap there. Let me move to (3,1) and trap at (2,2) — between Grid at (2,3) and my current area, cutting off approach.

Pulse(medic) is at dist=1 - adjacent, which means scan is blocked by Grid(vector) adjacency risk, but Grid is at dist=2 not adjacent to me. Pulse is at (2,4) dist=1 - I can scan Pulse to reveal their strategy and deal 1dmg. Grid is already scanned. Bolt is at dist=3, also scannable. Priority: scan Pulse first (adjacent, high value target as healer). I should also consider moving away from Pulse to avoid being adjacent next turn. Move to (4,4) to increase distance from Pulse and Grid, then scan Bolt at dist=3 from new position... wait, from (4,4) Bolt at (0,4) is dist=4, still in range. But scanning Pulse now at dist=1 is valuable - reveals healer strategy. Grid is at dist=2 so NOT adjacent to me currently, scan is not blocked. I'll scan Pulse (dist=1, in range) then move to (4,4) to reposition safely away from enemies.

Grid is wounded at (2,3) and adjacent to me at (2,4). I need to heal Grid. However, I must check if Snare vector is adjacent to me — Snare is at (3,1), dist=4, not adjacent, so patch is not blocked. Heal Grid first, then move away from Watcher who is close at (2,4) dist=2.

Grid is 2 tiles away at (2,3) — a threat I can move toward to intercept. Moving to (2,2) would be adjacent to Grid, letting me attack next turn, but there's a trap at (2,2). Moving to (3,3) keeps me between allies and enemies while staying fortified isn't possible if I move. Grid is the most immediate threat. I'll move to (2,3) isn't valid. Best option: move to (3,3) to advance toward enemies while staying near Snare at (3,1). Actually, staying put preserves Fortify (50% DR) which is valuable. No enemies in attack range. I'll wait to keep Fortify active.

All enemies are out of range (dist 4-6, max range 2). I'm at 2/2 HP so no need to retreat. Orders say maintain distance and use precision_shot on high-value targets. Watcher (oracle) is closest at dist=4. I need to close to within 2 tiles. Moving to (2,4) gets me closer to Watcher at (4,4) — dist would be 2, putting Watcher in range. But I moved, so precision_shot would deal 1dmg instead of 2. Still worth it. Let me check: from (2,4) to (4,4) = dist 2, in range. I should move there and shoot.

No enemies in range. Trap at (2,2) already exists. Grid is at (2,3), 3 tiles away. I should move closer to place another trap to control the approach. Move to (2,1) to stay out of Grid's denial range (adjacent only), then place a trap at (2,2) — wait, that's already trapped. Place trap at (3,3) to cover the corridor between Grid/Bolt and my allies Watcher(4,4) and Rampart(3,2). Move to (3,0) keeps distance; or move to (4,1) and trap (3,3) to block approach to Rampart. Let me move to (4,1) — no, that's away from enemies. Better: move to (3,3) and trap (2,3) to deny Grid's position area, but Grid is AT (2,3) so trap would be blocked (can't place on occupied tile). Move to (2,1) — adjacent to trap at (2,2), not adjacent to Grid at (2,3) since dist would be 2. From (2,1), trap (3,3) to block approach corridor to Rampart at (3,2). Actually trap range is ≤2 tiles, so from (2,1) I can reach (3,3) — dist=sqrt(1+4)=2.24, too far. From (3,3) I can trap (2,3) area but Grid is there. Move to (2,1), trap (2,3) — Grid is on (2,3), can't trap occupied. Move to (3,3) to threaten and trap (2,3)... no Grid is there. Best: move to (2,1), place trap at (3,1) my old position as fallback choke, or at (1,2) to cover left flank. Actually move to (3,3) and trap (2,2) already exists... From (3,3), trap (1,3) to slow Grid's movement — dist=2, valid empty tile.
actions.secondResult: Cannot use trap — adjacent to enemy Vector (Denial)

actions.thinking: I'm adjacent to Snare (enemy vector) at (3,3), which means my abilities are blocked by their Denial zone. I can only move or basic attack. Snare is right next to me - I should attack Snare to deal damage, then move away to break out of the Denial zone so I can use abilities next turn. Moving to (1,3) gets me away from Snare while staying near allies Bolt and Pulse.

Bolt targeted me last turn with precision_shot. I need to scan Bolt to deal 1 damage and reveal their prompt, then reposition. Pulse is wounded and already scanned. Grid is out of scan range. Scanning Bolt at dist=2 is valid. After scanning, I should move to maintain distance from Bolt who is advancing toward me.

I'm at 3/5 HP and patch is unavailable (0 uses left). Grid is at full HP so no healing needed. Snare is 3 tiles away - I need to stay safe. I should use overclock on Grid (vector) who can do the most with an extra action, then move to a safer position. Grid is at (1,3), adjacent to me at (1,4), so overclock is valid. After overclocking, I'll move away from the front to stay safe.

I'm overclocked (2 ability uses). Snare is at (3,3) dist=2, which means I can't place traps adjacent to them but I can place on tiles along enemy movement paths. Rampart at (3,2) will likely advance toward allies. Key chokepoints: (2,3) and (2,2) are likely paths. I should place traps on those tiles while staying out of Snare's denial range (adjacent). Current position (1,3) is safe at dist=2. I'll place two traps on likely enemy approach paths.

Grid is at (1,3), dist=2. There's already a trap at (2,2). I should place a trap at (2,3) to block Grid's path eastward, then stay at distance to maintain denial pressure without moving adjacent. I can't use trap if I move adjacent to Grid, so stay at (3,3) or move away. Place trap at (2,3) to cut off Grid's movement corridor between us.

No enemies in range. Fortified at (3,2) with 50% damage reduction. Grid is 3 tiles away at (1,3). I should hold position to maintain fortify bonus and use shield_wall toward the west where Grid is approaching. Snare is at (3,3) adjacent to me — I can shield_wall to protect them from Grid's western threat.

Both enemies are out of scan range from current position (Grid at dist 5, Pulse at dist 4 - need ≤4). Pulse is exactly 4 away but at (0,5) which is dist 4. I need to move closer to scan Pulse. Moving to (2,4) puts me at dist 3 from Pulse and I can scan. Avoid (2,2) and (2,3) which are trapped. Stay away from Grid (vector) to avoid denial. Recalibrate Snare after scanning to boost trap placement or attack effectiveness.

I'm at 1/5 HP and critically wounded. Patch is unavailable (0 uses). No sentinel to hide behind nearby. Watcher is at dist=3 and enemies are closing. I need to survive. Moving away from enemies is priority. Valid moves include (3,5) which puts distance. Grid needs heal but I can't patch. I can use overclock on Grid (dist=3, not adjacent) — need to get adjacent first. Moving to (1,4) gets me closer to Grid at (1,3) and away from Watcher at (2,4). Then I could overclock Grid next turn if I get adjacent. But at 1HP I'm extremely fragile. Best play: retreat to (0,2) or move toward Grid to overclock. Moving to (1,4) is 1 step from Grid at (1,3) — I could overclock Grid this turn if I move there and Grid is adjacent. (1,4) to (1,3) is dist=1, adjacent! So move to (1,4) then overclock Grid at (1,3).

Grid is at (1,3) distance 2 — I'm in denial range if they move adjacent. Traps already at (2,2) and (2,3) blocking that corridor. I should place a trap at (3,4) or (4,3) to cover another approach angle, then pulse Grid who is in range at dist 2... wait, pulse is only 1-tile AoE so Grid at dist 2 is out of pulse range. Best play: move to (4,3) to maintain distance from Grid while staying out of denial range, then place a trap at (3,3) my current position to cover the corridor behind me, or place at (3,4) to block northern approach to Watcher.

I'm overclocked with 2 ability uses. Watcher(oracle) is at (2,4), dist=2, in range. Traps at (2,3) and (2,2) already placed. I should place a new trap on a likely enemy path. Rampart is at (3,2) moving north, likely path through (2,2) already trapped. Snare is at (4,3) — likely moves west toward us, path through (3,3). I can't use trap if adjacent to enemy vector Snare, but Snare is 3 tiles away so I'm safe. Place trap at (3,3) to intercept Snare's approach, and trap at (2,4) or attack Watcher. Wait — (2,3) already has a trap. Watcher is AT (2,4), I can attack for 1 dmg. Use attack on Watcher at (2,4) with ability, then place trap at (3,3) for Snare's likely path.

Currently fortified at (3,2) with full HP. No enemies in range. Grid is 3 tiles west at (1,3), Pulse is 4 tiles away at (1,4). Trap at (3,4) and (2,3) block some paths. Moving to (2,3) would hit a trap. Best to advance toward (2,2) — moves me 1 tile closer to enemies while staying between them and allies Watcher@(2,4) and Snare@(4,3). But (2,2) is a trap tile too. Moving to (3,1) or staying put. Actually I should hold the front line — moving to (2,3) is a trap. Moving to (3,3) keeps me central and closer to enemy but (3,4) is trap. Stay at current position to maintain fortify bonus and hold the line, or advance to (2,2)... that's a trap. Valid moves include (3,3) — 1 tile north puts me between Grid@(1,3) and allies. Move to (3,3) to advance front line, still not adjacent to Grid so fortify won't apply next turn if I move. Actually staying fortified is strong with 50% DR. I'll wait to maintain fortify.

Pulse is at critical HP and adjacent to me — but I can't attack adjacent enemies effectively with scan (scan needs ≤4 tiles but Pulse is only 1 tile away, that's fine range-wise). However, Grid is 2 tiles away and would block scan if I move adjacent. Pulse is critical and already scanned. I should move away from Pulse to avoid adjacency issues and then scan or attack. Actually, Pulse is at (1,4) which is 1 tile away — I can scan it from here (dist=1, within range 4). But Grid is at (1,3) which is 2 tiles away — not adjacent, so scan isn't blocked. Priority: Pulse is critical, hitting it with scan deals 1 dmg and could finish it or leave it near death. But I already know Pulse's prompt. Better to move to safety first (away from potential trap tiles — (2,3) and (3,4) are trapped). Move to (2,5) to stay safe and maintain range, then scan Pulse to deal damage since it's critical.

All enemies are 3 tiles away. Existing traps at (2,3), (2,2), (3,3) already cover some approach paths. Snare is at (4,3) moving east(?), Rampart at (3,2) moving north, Watcher at (2,5) moving north. I should place a trap on a likely approach tile. Moving to (1,2) or staying and trapping (2,4) could catch Watcher coming down. Actually, Watcher is at (2,5) heading north — unlikely to come south. Rampart at (3,2) moving north might step on (3,3) which is already trapped. Snare at (4,3) moving east, away from me. I should move toward center to threaten and place a trap on an approach corridor. Move to (2,4) to get closer and trap (3,4) which is between Snare and Watcher positions.

I'm fortified at (3,2) with 6/6 HP. Grid(vector) is at (2,4), dist=3, not in range. Snare is at (4,3), Watcher at (2,5). Trap at (3,4) blocks direct advance north. I should hold the front line between allies and Grid. Moving to (3,3) advances me toward Grid while staying between allies. Avoid (2,3) trap. At (3,3) I'd be dist=2 from Grid — not adjacent so no denial issue. I lose fortify if I move, but positioning is important. Actually, I'm already fortified and Grid is far — staying put preserves fortify bonus and holds line. Wait to maintain fortify and front line position.

Grid is at (2,4) dist=3, not adjacent so trap is available. Trap at (3,4) already exists. I should place a trap to create a chokepoint. Moving to (4,4) then trapping (3,4) is blocked since trap already there. Let me move to (5,3) to maintain distance from Grid while placing a trap at (4,4) or (3,3) to cover approaches. Actually, valid moves include (4,4) and (3,3). Grid is moving north from (2,4), so (3,4) already trapped. I'll move to (4,4) to be closer and place a trap at (3,4)... already trapped. Better: move to (3,3) to tighten denial range on Grid if they advance, then trap (2,3) - but (2,3) is a valid move tile meaning it's empty. Wait, trap targets enemy paths. Grid at (2,4) heading N might go to (2,5) toward Watcher. I should trap (2,3) or (3,3) to block southern approach. I'll move to (4,4) (safe, no traps there) then place trap at (3,4)... already placed. Trap at (4,5) instead to cover that flank. Actually best: stay, trap at (3,4) - already exists. Move to (3,3) then trap (2,3) to block Grid's path.

I'm adjacent to Grid's DENIAL zone, so scan is blocked. I must move away first. Grid is at (2,4) directly south. I need to escape the denial zone and then scan or recalibrate. Moving to (3,5) gets me away from Grid and avoids the traps at (2,2), (2,3), (3,4). From (3,5) I'm 2 tiles from Grid - scan should work. Snare is my damage dealer at (3,3) - recalibrate could help but scan is higher priority per orders. From (3,5), Grid at (2,4) is dist=sqrt(1+1)=1.4, within range 4. I'll move to (3,5) then scan Grid to deal 1 damage and re-confirm their strategy.

I'm at (2,4) with 4HP. Snare is at (3,3) dist=2, Watcher at (3,5) dist=2. Traps already at (2,3), (2,2), (3,4). Snare is moving west so may step onto (2,3) trap. Watcher at (3,5) may move south toward (3,4) trap. I should place a trap at (3,3) or nearby to threaten Snare's position, but Snare is at (3,3) so I can't trap occupied tile. Tile (4,4) is valid move and in range - could place trap there. Better: stay put and trap (3,4) is already covered. Let me trap (4,3) - wait, need to check range ≤2. From (2,4): (4,3) is dist=sqrt(4+1)=2.24, out of range. From (2,4): (3,3) is occupied. (4,4) is reachable by move but then trap from there... Move to (1,4) to stay safe from denial, then trap (3,4)? But (3,4) already has a trap. Move to (1,5) and trap (2,5) or (3,5) where Watcher is? Watcher is at (3,5), can't trap occupied. Trap (2,5) from (1,5) - dist=1, valid. That could catch Watcher moving west. Actually stay at (2,4), trap (3,4) already placed. Trap (4,4) from current pos - dist=2, valid empty tile on enemy's likely path.

Grid is at (1,4) dist=3, wounded. I need to control approach paths. Traps already at (2,2),(2,3),(3,4). I should place a trap at (2,4) to block Grid's likely approach path toward my allies. Move to (4,3) to stay away from Grid's denial range while maintaining area control, then place trap at (3,4)... wait, (3,4) already has a trap. Let me move to (4,4) to extend denial coverage and place trap at (3,4) — already trapped. Better: move to (2,4) would be risky near Grid. Stay put or move to (4,4), then trap (2,4) from range... (2,4) is within range-2 from (3,3). Place trap at (2,4) first to block Grid's advance, then move to (4,3) to maintain positioning away from denial range.

Grid is at (1,4) distance 4, out of attack range. I'm fortified at (3,2) facing N. Allies Snare is at (4,3) and Watcher at (3,5). Grid is to the NW. I should advance toward Grid while staying between allies and enemies. Moving to (2,3) puts me closer to Grid and between my allies and the threat, but there's a trap at (2,3). Moving to (3,3) advances north without trap risk and maintains frontline position. From (3,3) I can shield_wall west to protect against Grid's approach.

Grid is wounded at (1,4), 3 tiles away — within scan range and already scanned. I should attack if possible, but attack only works adjacent. I can't attack from range. Grid is wounded so scan would add pressure. I already know Grid's prompt. Best use: scan Grid again for 1 damage to help finish it off (it's wounded). Check traps — (3,4) is trapped, so I must avoid moving there. Valid moves exclude trapped tiles? (3,4) is in valid moves list but it's trapped — avoid it. I'll stay at (3,5) and scan Grid at (1,4) for 1 damage. Grid is 3 tiles away, within scan range of 4. This deals damage without closing to adjacent range.

I'm fortified at (3,3) with 50% damage reduction. Grid is 3 tiles away at (1,4), critical HP. I should hold position to maintain fortify passive. Grid can't reach me this turn. I'll use shield_wall toward the west to protect allies from Grid's direction, then wait to keep fortify active.

Grid is at (1,4) distance 4, critical HP - not in range yet. Traps already at (2,2),(2,3),(3,4),(2,3),(2,4) - good coverage around (2,3) area. Need to close distance to get Grid in range. Move to (3,4) puts me closer but there's a trap there. Move to (4,4) or (3,2) are safe. Grid is moving west away from me - need to reposition. Move to (3,4) is blocked by trap. Let me move to (4,4) to stay mobile, then place a trap at (3,4) area... wait (3,4) already has a trap. Move to (3,2) to reposition toward Grid's likely path, place trap at (2,2) area - already covered. Move to (4,4) and place trap at (3,4) - already trapped. Best: move to (3,4) - actually that has a trap, can I move there? Valid moves include [3,4]. Place trap somewhere useful - maybe (2,5) to cut off northern approach. Actually move to (3,4) first (valid), then trap at (2,4) - already trapped. Trap at (2,5) to extend coverage northward.

HP is critically low at 2/8. Snare is at (3,4) distance 2 — must not move adjacent or my abilities get blocked. Traps at (2,3) and (2,2) already cover southern approach. I should place a trap on (2,4) to block the direct corridor between me and Snare, then back away west to (0,4) to create distance. This forces enemies through trapped tiles.

Grid is at distance 4 and out of scan range from current position. Need to close distance to scan. Moving to (2,4) would put me at distance 2 from Grid, but (2,4) has a trap. Moving to (1,4) puts me at distance 1 from Grid but adjacent to enemy Vector - forbidden. Moving to (2,5) has a trap. Best safe move toward Grid: (2,3) is dist ~2.8 but has trap. (4,4) keeps distance but Grid is already scanned. Since Grid is already scanned and at critical HP, I should recalibrate Snare at (3,4) to boost their effectiveness against Grid. Snare is adjacent to me at (3,4). I can recalibrate Snare, then wait or reposition.

firstAction.addendum: Grid is at (0,4) critical HP and moving west. Intercept and finish them. Avoid traps at (2,4) and (2,5).

actions.thinking: Grid is at (0,4) critical HP moving west. I'm at (3,4). I need to close distance to get in range. Moving to (1,4) would put me adjacent and trigger denial, so move to (2,4) — wait, that has a trap. Move to (2,3) instead — but that also has a trap. Valid moves show (2,4) and (2,3) are listed but they have traps. Orders say avoid (2,4) and (2,5). So best approach: move to (1,4) gets me adjacent to Grid at (0,4) allowing attack, but denial risk. Actually at (1,4) I'd be adjacent to Grid and could attack for 1dmg to finish them. Denial only blocks some abilities from enemies, not my own actions against them. Move to (1,4) then attack Grid at (0,4).

Grid is at (0,4) critical but dist=4, out of my attack range. Snare is at (1,4) to my west. Grid is threatening Snare. I should move toward Grid to intercept/protect Snare, and position to shield_wall. Moving to (2,4) would put me adjacent to Snare and closer to Grid. But (2,4) has a trap. Move to (2,3) also has a trap. Valid move to (1,3) puts me closer. Actually, best is to intercept Grid to protect Snare - intercept has range ≤2 tiles. Snare is at (1,4), dist=3 from me so outside intercept range. I'll move to (2,4)... but that's a trap. Move to (3,4) is valid but also has a trap listed. Let me check: valid tiles include (3,4). Traps at (3,4) - risky. Move to (2,3) is listed valid but trap there too. I'll move to (1,3) to get closer to Snare/Grid, then shield_wall West to protect Snare from Grid.

At 1HP I'm critically vulnerable. Snare is adjacent at (1,4) with DENIAL active, blocking my trap ability. I can still attack or move. Pulse is also likely blocked by DENIAL. Best option: attack Snare for 1 damage, then move away to (0,5) to escape the denial zone and survive. Moving first then attacking won't help since I'd still be in range. Attack first, then retreat north to (0,5).
